title: ADV Strikes Again
wikitext:
[]: ADV Strikes Again             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Sep 03, 2004 3:09 pm I recently received an e-mail from www.amazon.com that stated, among other things, that a large percentage of the people who purchased Neon Genesis Evangelion: Perfect Collection also bought the first platinum edition DVD. It seems ADV was correct in assuming fans who already own the old DVDs would buy the new ones. Go ADV! W00t! W00t!

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Sep 03, 2004 4:41 pm They didn't get me though

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Sep 03, 2004 5:21 pm Oh they will. They will.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Sep 03, 2004 5:43 pm They got me. Damn them and their shiny boxes.

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Fri Sep 03, 2004 5:49 pm theyre gonna ger me once i aquire cash Y_Y....after HL2 if its pushed up....

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Sep 03, 2004 5:54 pm I'd like to read exactly what this e-mail said.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Sep 03, 2004 5:58 pm The relevant section is: Amazon wrote: Dear Amazon.com Customer, We've noticed that customers who have purchased Neon Genesis Evangelion - Perfect Collection also purchased Neon Genesis Evangelion - Platinum Collection 2 on DVD. For this reason, you might like to know that Neon Genesis Evangelion - Platinum Collection 2 will be released on September 21, 2004 on DVD. You can pre-order yours at a savings of 18% by following the link below.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Sep 03, 2004 6:05 pm Ha. Well, it's not like we had any choice before Platinum. Buying the Perfect Collection was really the only way many of us could ever see the series (until ABC goes out on a limb and picks it up). I wouldn't have bought Platinum if the Perfect Collection wasn't so, you know... crappy.

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Fri Sep 03, 2004 6:12 pm Fools. You're all a bunch of corporate tools! :P

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Sep 03, 2004 8:23 pm coff wrote: Fools. You're all a bunch of corporate tools! :P

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Fri Sep 03, 2004 8:32 pm Amen to that, Hunter21. I'd like to get it, but am currently broke at the moment. I've got the whole series on DVD already, but I'd like the audio commentary with Spike Spencer. :mrgreen: Hooray for audio commentary!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Sep 03, 2004 8:37 pm I thoroughly enjoyed the Audio Commentary. Turns out Spike did the voice of my favorite non-eva character and I hadn't even noticed! (In case your wondering, the character is Gojo Shioji from Excel Saga.)

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Sep 03, 2004 11:38 pm I really hope somebody finds that radio ad that he and Tiffany did together.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sat Sep 04, 2004 12:23 am A saving of 18 % , thats not bad you know

[]:             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Sat Sep 04, 2004 9:12 pm coff wrote: Fools. You're all a bunch of corporate tools! :P

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Sat Sep 04, 2004 9:19 pm Dave wrote: I thoroughly enjoyed the Audio Commentary. Turns out Spike did the voice of my favorite non-eva character and I hadn't even noticed! (In case your wondering, the character is Gojo Shioji from Excel Saga.)

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Tue Sep 14, 2004 4:48 pm ShoKenju wrote: theyre gonna ger me once i aquire cash Y_Y....after HL2 if its pushed up....

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Oct 03, 2004 2:34 am Dave wrote: I thoroughly enjoyed the Audio Commentary. Turns out Spike did the voice of my favorite non-eva character and I hadn't even noticed! (In case your wondering, the character is Gojo Shioji from Excel Saga.)

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sun Oct 03, 2004 1:00 pm arent they still ridiculously expensive?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Oct 03, 2004 1:12 pm arent they still ridiculously expensive?
summary:
[]: ADV Strikes Again             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Sep 03, 2004 3:09 pm I recently received an e-mail from www.amazon.com that stated, among other things, that a large percentage of the people who purchased Neon Genesis Evangelion: Perfect Collection also bought the first platinum edition DVD. It seems ADV was correct in assuming fans who already own the old DVDs would buy the new ones. Go ADV! W00t! W00t!
